A new abstraction: FutureIndices, current implementation uses a hacky way to process the future indices, i.e., storing them in the input_ids of ScheduleBatch and using negative numbers to identify which ones are futures.


FutureIndices should always be the only output of the forward stream.

sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
original worker health check had a small bug where when workers return healthy, it sleeps anyway
this pr consolidate the health check into a single loop

fetch all unhealthy worker
send health check in parallel
fetch unhealthy workers -> return if empty
sleep otherwise

sglang.title: Optimize copy_kv_cache for spec decoding
sglang.author: YAMY1234
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The original KV cache copy kernel used a 1D inner loop over stride bytes.
While correct, it left memory bandwidth underutilized and introduced overhead from the inner loop. We want to improve both performance and clarity, while keeping the in-place copy semantics 100% safe.
Modifications

vllm.title: fix(v1/kv_cache): resolve async KV transfer bug in cascade attention
vllm.author: ayushsatyam146
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Solves #23130. This change fixes a critical bug in vLLM's cascade attention optimization in the V1 arch. The bug is in get_num_common_prefix_blocks(), which determines how many KV cache blocks are shared among all currently running requests to enable cascade attention optimizations.
Changes

vllm.title: fix[DP][v1]: Prevent hangs from mismatched worker configurations
vllm.author: ayushsatyam146
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fixes #26187. Previously, vLLM could hang when the leader and workers had different EPLB
configurations (e.g., --enable-eplb-log-balancedness), causing only some
workers to attempt All-Reduce calls.

Extended parallel config hash to include EPLB parameters
Added config hash exchange during
